pageTitle: SVG Cleaner - Remove XML Declaration & DOCTYPE for Free | GetNiftyKit
metaDescription: Free tool that strips the XML declaration, DOCTYPE, and leading comments from SVG files, leaving pure SVG code. Fixes SVG upload failures on Blogger and other platforms — processed entirely in your browser, no server upload.
softwareAppDescription: A free tool that removes the XML declaration, DOCTYPE, and leading comments from SVG files, leaving clean, pure SVG code.
subtitle: Strip the XML declaration, DOCTYPE, and comments, and keep only pure SVG code
taglineExtra: When SVG uploads fail on Blogger and similar platforms, this removes exactly the metadata causing it.
dropzoneText: Drag and drop your SVG files here, or click to choose
unsupportedFileError: Only SVG files can be added.
invalidSvgError: This isn't a valid SVG file — no <svg> tag was found.
alreadyCleanText: Already a pure SVG — nothing to remove.
pasteInvalidError: This isn't valid SVG code — no <svg> tag was found.

faq.q: Why won't my SVG upload to Blogger or some CMS platforms?
faq.a: Some platforms reject SVG files that start with an XML declaration (<?xml version="1.0"?>), a <!DOCTYPE svg ...> declaration, or leading comments. SVGs exported from icon sites like svgrepo.com and Flaticon, or from illustration software, almost always include this metadata automatically, which is why the problem shows up so often.

faq.q: Does this tool damage the actual artwork in my SVG?
faq.a: No. It only removes the XML declaration, DOCTYPE, and leading comments — the shapes, paths, and colors inside the <svg> tag are left completely untouched.

faq.q: Can I clean multiple files at once?
faq.a: Yes. Upload several SVG files together and each one is cleaned independently, with its own save button.

faq.q: Is my uploaded SVG sent to a server?
faq.a: No. Everything is processed entirely in your browser — files are never uploaded or stored anywhere.

faq.q: What happens if I upload an already-clean SVG?
faq.a: You'll just see a message that there's nothing to remove, and you can still save an identical copy if you'd like.

infoIntroP: SVG Cleaner is a free tool that strips the XML declaration (<?xml version="1.0"?>), DOCTYPE declaration, and leading comments from the top of an SVG file — metadata that isn't needed for the actual artwork but breaks uploads on some platforms. SVG files exported from tools like Illustrator, Figma, svgrepo.com, or Flaticon often include this metadata automatically, and platforms like Blogger can flatly reject an image upload if it's present. This tool picks out exactly that problematic code and removes it while leaving the real artwork untouched.
infoHowtoP: Drag and drop the SVG files you want to clean, or use the choose file button — you can upload several at once. Each file is analyzed and cleaned automatically the moment it's added, showing exactly what was removed (XML declaration, DOCTYPE, leading comments) and how the file size changed. Click Save to download the cleaned file under its original name. If you only have a code snippet rather than a file, expand "Paste code directly" below to clean text instead.
infoUseCases: When an SVG icon from svgrepo.com or Flaticon won't upload to Blogger
When an SVG exported from Illustrator or Inkscape won't preview on a particular CMS
When you want to strip unnecessary metadata before pasting SVG code inline on a webpage
When you need to clean up a batch of SVG icon files at once
privacyNote: This tool processes everything in your browser — your uploaded SVG files are never sent to or stored on a server.
